stem: A software testing study surveys 20 test cases and observes 7 positive outcomes out of 20 (35.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (10 observations, rate = 30.0%) and Group B (10 observations, rate = 40.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 36% and 51% of the population. Estimate the true software bug rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.35. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.3368 (range [0.325, 0.3471]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.1812, 0.5671]. Point estimate: 0.3368.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.43: 0.43×0.253 + 0.57×0.4 = 0.3368.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
